name: H2O.ai API
what_it_does: Open-source AutoML and machine learning platform with REST API for automated model training, feature engineering, explainability, and model deployment at scale.
use_cases: Automatically training and selecting best ML models for tabular data from agent pipelines
Explaining model predictions with SHAP and LIME-based explanations via API
Deploying trained H2O models as MOJO/POJO artifacts for low-latency inference
Running distributed ML training on large datasets via H2O Sparkling Water
Comparing model performance across experiments for ML experiment tracking
not_for: Deep learning with custom neural architectures (use PyTorch/TensorFlow instead)
Organizations without data science expertise to configure and interpret results
Real-time inference with sub-millisecond latency requirements
best_when: An agent needs to train high-quality models on tabular/structured data with minimal ML expertise and built-in explainability.
avoid_when: You need custom neural network architectures or unstructured data (text, images, audio) modeling.

auth.notes: H2O-3 open-source: minimal auth on local instance. H2O AI Cloud: token-based API keys with user/group permissions. Enterprise features include LDAP/AD integration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: H2O-3 open-source: completely free, unlimited use
pricing.paid_tiers: H2O AI Cloud: enterprise pricing from $500k+/year; H2O.ai Driverless AI: $100k+/year
pricing.notes: H2O-3 AutoML is Apache 2.0 open-source and free. Enterprise products (Driverless AI, H2O AI Cloud) are commercial with very high price points. Most developers use H2O-3 directly.

performance.rate_limits: Limited by cluster resources; no external API rate limits for open-source version

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation exists but the Python SDK is the primary interface. Error messages from H2O backend are sometimes Java stack traces that require interpretation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Model training jobs are created with unique IDs. Repeated runs with same data produce similar (not identical) models due to randomness. Job status polling needed for completion detection.
known_agent_gotchas: H2O cluster must be running before any API calls — cluster startup is a separate step
Training is async — agents must poll for job completion using job IDs
Memory requirements for large datasets can exceed cluster RAM — must size cluster appropriately
Model serialization (MOJO/POJO) required for deployment; not handled by standard REST patterns

security_score_components.security_notes: Open-source H2O-3 has minimal security by design (local cluster assumption). Enterprise versions add TLS, LDAP, and audit logging. Security posture depends heavily on deployment configuration.
